one_liner: A 15-year-old girl is brought in for **short stature** and **primary amenorrhea**, and physical exam reveals a **webbed neck** and a **wide carrying angle**.

stage2.hpi_extension: The patient has always been one of the shortest in her class; growth velocity has been below the 3rd percentile since age 5. She has not yet menstruated and has minimal breast development. There has been no appreciable growth spurt. Parents recall that as a newborn she had marked **puffiness of the hands and feet**. She has a history of recurrent otitis media in childhood.
stage2.pmh: Neonatal lymphedema of hands and feet. Multiple episodes of otitis media. Known bicuspid aortic valve noted on newborn echo ordered because of a murmur.
stage2.family_history: Average parental height (mother 165 cm, father 180 cm). No known genetic disease.
stage2.social_history: Lives with both parents. 8th grade student, average performance.
stage2.physical_exam: General: **short (height <3rd percentile)**, prepubertal appearance. HEENT: **low posterior hairline, webbed neck (pterygium colli), low-set ears, high-arched palate**. Chest: **shield chest with widely spaced nipples**, Tanner 1 breasts. Cardiovascular: **systolic ejection click** at the right upper sternal border; delayed and weak femoral pulses (upper extremity BP > lower extremity BP). Extremities: **increased carrying angle (cubitus valgus)**, **short 4th metacarpals**, edema of the dorsa of feet. Pelvic: Tanner 1 pubic hair, no secondary sexual development.

lab_results.result: Peripheral blood karyotype: **45,X** in 40 of 40 cells (or mosaic 45,X/46,XX). **Barr body absent** on buccal smear.
lab_results.clinical_significance: **45,X karyotype (monosomy X)** confirms **Turner syndrome**. Loss of a second sex chromosome leads to **streak ovaries**, short stature (haploinsufficiency of the **SHOX** gene), and a characteristic somatic phenotype.

lab_results.result: **Estradiol markedly decreased**, **LH and FSH markedly elevated** (hypergonadotropic hypogonadism). Anti-Mullerian hormone undetectable. Free T4 and TSH within normal limits (baseline).
lab_results.clinical_significance: **Low estradiol with elevated LH and FSH** confirms **primary ovarian failure** from streak ovaries and explains the primary amenorrhea and delayed puberty.

lab_results.result: Transthoracic echocardiogram: **bicuspid aortic valve** and **coarctation of the aorta** at the juxtaductal region. Renal ultrasound: **horseshoe kidney**.
lab_results.clinical_significance: Cardiac anomalies (bicuspid aortic valve, coarctation of the aorta) and **horseshoe kidney** are part of the Turner syndrome spectrum. Aortic root dilation and dissection are feared long-term complications.

stage5.final_clue: She is started on **growth hormone therapy** to maximize final adult height and will later begin **low-dose estrogen replacement** to induce puberty followed by cyclic estrogen-progesterone. Cardiology follow-up and serial echocardiograms are planned for her bicuspid aortic valve and coarctation.

debrief.classic_presentation: Turner syndrome classically presents as a girl with **short stature, primary amenorrhea, webbed neck, shield chest with widely spaced nipples, low posterior hairline, wide carrying angle, and short 4th metacarpals**. Neonates may have **lymphedema of the hands and feet** and cystic hygroma, which in utero often appears as increased nuchal translucency. Turner syndrome is the **most common cause of primary amenorrhea** and should be suspected in any short prepubertal girl, especially with dysmorphic features.
debrief.pathophysiology: Turner syndrome is caused by complete or partial absence of one **X chromosome**, most commonly **45,X monosomy** (paternal X loss is most frequent) or mosaicism (45,X/46,XX). **Haploinsufficiency of the SHOX gene** accounts for short stature and skeletal features. Ovarian germ cells undergo accelerated atresia, producing **streak ovaries** and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism. Associated anomalies include **bicuspid aortic valve, coarctation of the aorta, horseshoe kidney**, and increased risk of autoimmune thyroiditis.
debrief.treatment_overview: **Recombinant growth hormone** is used in childhood to maximize final adult height. **Estrogen replacement therapy** is initiated around age 12-13 to induce secondary sexual characteristics, followed by combined estrogen-progesterone to maintain endometrial health. Lifelong surveillance includes serial echocardiograms (for aortic root dilation and dissection risk), thyroid function testing (Hashimoto thyroiditis), audiology, and screening for celiac disease. **Pregnancy is possible only with donor oocytes** and is considered high-risk.
high_yield_pearls: Turner syndrome = **45,XO**; most common cause of **primary amenorrhea** in a short girl.
Classic phenotype: **short stature, webbed neck, shield chest, widely spaced nipples, cubitus valgus, short 4th metacarpals**.
Cardiac: **bicuspid aortic valve and coarctation of the aorta**; renal: **horseshoe kidney**.
Hormone pattern: **low estrogen, high LH and FSH** (hypergonadotropic hypogonadism from streak ovaries).
**No Barr body** (only one X chromosome); contrast with Klinefelter 47,XXY where a Barr body is present.
sources: First Aid for the USMLE Step 1 (2017) — Reproductive chapter, 'Turner syndrome' section.
First Aid for the USMLE Step 1 (2017) — Reproductive chapter, 'Primary amenorrhea and disorders of sex development'.
